title: quercus runcinatifolia var. typica
intro: Quercus runcinatifolia var. typica, also known as the 'typical oak-leafed quercus', is a specific variety of the Quercus runcinatifolia tree, a species of evergreen tree native to the Himalayan region. The medicinal properties of Quercus runcinatifolia var. typica are attributed to its leaves, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of this plant are numerous, including the potential treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties, as well as its ability to help manage diabetes and cardiovascular diseases. Quercus runcinatifolia var. typica is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its effects.
